name: Self Employed Health Insurance
description: The self-employed health insurance deduction can help offset some of the costs of paying out-of-pocket for health care coverage for you, your spouse, your dependents, and children under age 27.

qualification_criteria.question: Do you meet the definition of self employed? You meet the definition of self employed if you (1) carry on a trade or business as a sole proprietor or an independent contractor, (2) are a member of a partnership that carries on a trade or business, (3) are a member of an LLC classified as a partnership for tax purposes, or (4) are a shareholder of 2% or more of the stock of an S-Corp.
Did you have earned income from your business? The Self Employed Health Insurance deduction cannot exceed the earned income from your business.

Were you, your spouse, or any of your dependents eligible to participate in an employer-subsidized health plan for any month that you paid your premium? The self employed health insurance deduction is only available for months when neither you nor your spouse nor your dependents were eligible to participate in an employer-subsidized health plan.

Did you pay any of the following premiums for yourself, your spouse, your dependents, and your children under age 27 even if they were not dependents: (1) medical insurance, (2) dental and vision insurance, (3) qualified long-term care insurance, or (4) Medicare? If you qualify, you will be able to deduct 100% of your premiums for health insurance, dental and vision insurance, and Medicare. There are limited deductions for qualified long-term care insurance.

implementation_details.time_to_implement: 1-3 hours to complete paperwork for insurance policies

key_dates.deadline: The open enrollment period for signing up for new health insurance runs from  November 1 to January 15, absent a qualifying event.

action_items.description: Purchase health insurance through the individual health insurance market or through the federal or state exchange marketplace
If you are a more-than-2% shareholder of an S-Corp, and the S-Corp pays for the health insurance premiums, make sure that the S-Corp includes the premiums in income on your W-2.

tax_filing_by_entity.details: 1040, Schedule 1 (Part II) | 17 | Tax Advisor

state_considerations: Some states have different open enrollment deadlines for signing up for new health insurance, absent a qualifying life event.

name: Itemized Deduction
description: Take itemized tax deductions on your tax return to reduce taxable income.

qualification_criteria.question: Do you have large unreimbursed medical and dental expenses? If you have incurred qualified out-of-pocket medical or dental expenses that are not covered by insurance, you may be able to deduct those expenses from your taxable income. The deduction for medical and dental expenses is limited to 7.5% of adjusted gross income.

Do you pay a large amount of mortgage interest or real property taxes on your home? If you paid interest on a home mortgage or home equity line of credit, or paid real property taxes on your home, you may be able to deduct those expenses from your taxable income. The total of the state and local income tax deduction is limited to $10,000.

Do you have large uninsured casualty or theft losses from a Federally declared disaster? If you lost your home, vehicles, or other personal property in a Federally declared disaster, you may be able to deduct the value of those losses from your taxable income. Personal casualty and theft losses from a federally delared disaster are subject to a $500/casualty limitation.

Do you pay state and local income, sales, or personal property taxes? If you paid a large amount of local taxes, including state income taxes, and state sales taxes, you may be able to deduct those expenses from your taxable income. The total of the state and local income tax deduction is limited to $10,000.
Do you make large contributions to charities? If you made large contributions to a qualified charity, you may be able to deduct those expenses from your taxable income.

Are your itemized deductions greater than the standard deduction? If your itemized deductions are greater than the standard deduction, you will qualify to take the itemized deduction to reduce your taxable income.

Is there any reason that you are prohibited from taking the standard deduction? If your itemized deductions are less than the standard deduction, but you are prohibited from taking the standard deduction, you may take the itemized deduction. You cannot take the standard deduction if (1) you are a married individual filing as married filing separately whose spouse itemizes deductions, (2) you are an individual who files a tax return for a period of less than 12 months because of a change in your annual accounting period, or (3) you are an individual who was a nonresident alien or a dual-status alien during the year and did not choose to be treated as a U.S. resident for tax purposes while married to a U.S. Citizen or alien resident.

action_items.description: Document unreimbursed medical and dental expenses
Pay interest to a mortgage holder. Obtain IRS Form 1098
Pay taxes on your real property to local tax jurisdicition
Document unreimbursed casualty or theft losses from a federally declared disaster. If you had insurance, file an insurance claim and reduce the deduction by the amount of the insurance reimbursement.
Obtain the disaster declaration number assigned by FEMA
Obtain a qualified appraisal to determine the fair market value of property affected by a federally declared disaster or theft
Make and document contributions to qualified charitable organizations
Include gambling winnings as earned income that income can be reduced by applicable gambling losses.

tax_filing_by_entity.details: 1040 or 1040SR - Schedule A | 2022-01-17 00:00:00 | Pub. 51 | Tax Advisor
1040 or 1040SR - Form 8283 | Tax Advisor | Required if noncash charitable contributions are over $500
1040 or 1040SR - Form 4684 | Tax Advisor | Required for casualty and theft losses from a federally declared disaster

state_considerations: Most states allow itemized deductions to determine state taxable income. Some states only allow the taxpayer to itemize deductions if they itemized on their federal return. Other states do not recognize federal itemized deductions but allow state-specific itemzied deductions. Lastly, other states do not allow any itemized deductions.

description: S Corporation election enables income above the reasonable compensation to be exempt from self-employment tax. Late election allows a retroactive implementation of this tax strategy.

qualification_criteria.question: Does this business have fewer than 100 shareholders?
Are the shareholders of this business US residents?
Is this business a US domestic company?
Is there at least one class of stock with pro rata distributions?
Did the business fail to qualify as an S corporation solely because the election was not timely?
Did the business and all shareholders reported their income consistent with an S corporation election in effect for the year the election should have been made and all subsequent years?
Does the business plan to make the election for the years earlier than 3 years and 75 days since the effective date of the proposed election (if not already a C Corp)?

implementation_details.third_party_details: Tax advisory should aid in completing the form

action_items.description: Prepare Form 2553

Craft a reasonable cause statement for failing to file form 2553 by the original due date of the S corp election.
Send to client to review and sign reasonable cause statement.
Craft a reasonable cause shareholder's statement
Send to all client shareholders (Or one letter per shareholder) to review and sign reasonable cause shareholder statement.
Confirm client signed resonable cause statement.

Gather the Late S Election package documents to send to the IRS:
1. Form 2553 with title written "Filed Pursuant to Rev. Proc. 2013-30"
2. Include signed reasonable cause statement on behalf of the business
3. Include signed reasonable cause shareholders statement on behalf of the shareholders
Mail Late S Election package with reasonable cause statement on the top of the packet with confirmation of receipt by mail.

action_items.description:
Create & properly setup the business entity (if not already done so).

- Identify business name
- Register "doing business as" in the state of operations
- Prepare Articles of Incorporation
- Prepare bylaws for business entity
- Prepare board minutes for all shareholder meetings.
- Register for an EIN number with the IRS
- Obtain business licenses/permits as required by local, county, state and federal business laws.
- Determine the tax year of this entity
Prepare Form 2553 with the IRS
Obtain signatures of all shareholders on Form 2553.
Mail S Corp election form with confirmation of receipt by mail to the IRS office.
Obtain confirmation letter and store in safe client file for future record keeping purposes
Determine proper reasonable compensation for shareholder employees.
Setup payroll provider to being payroll process of salary compensation for all entity employees.

name: R&D Tax Credit
description: The credit may help with the cost of research & development.

qualification_criteria.question: Can you describe in detail what new or improved product or process you created for increasing performance, finction, reliability, or quality? To be eligiable for R&D tax credit, the research must be intended to be useful in the development of a new or improved business component of the taxpayer (knowen as the business component test or qualified purpose test).

Can you describe in detail how you have attempted to eliminate uncertainty about the development or improvment of a product or process? To be eligiable for R&D tax credit, the research must be intended to discover information that would eliminate technological uncertainty (e.g., capability, method or the appropriate design) related to the development or improvement of a product or process.

Can you describe in detail your process of experimentation and how it relies on engineering, physics, chemistry, biology, or computer science? To be eligiable for R&D tax credit, the research undertaken for the purpose of discovering information must  be technological in nature that fundamentally relies on technological principles of engineering, physical or biological science, or computer sciences?

Can you describe in detail your method of experimentation (e.g., modeling, simulation, systematic trial and error or other methods) and how you evaluated alternatives for achieving your desired result? To be eligiable for R&D tax credit, the research must substantially constitute elements of a process of experimentation including identifying technological uncertainty and one or more alternatives to eliminate it, and conducting a process of evaluating the alternatives?

Did you conduct R&D activities that are not excluded from the definition of qualified research? The following activities are excluded from the definition of qualified research: 1) research conducted after the beginning of commercial production of the business component. The following activities are deemed to occur after the commencement of commercial production: a) pre-production planning for a finished product; b) tooling up for production; c) trial production runs; d) troubleshooting involving detecting faults in production equipment or processes; e) accumulating data relating to production processes, and f) debugging flaws in the product; 2) adaptation of existing business components; 3) duplication of an existing business component; 4) surveys, studies, and research relating to management functions; 5) internal use software; 6) research conducted outside the United States, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, or any U.S. possession; 7) research in the social sciences, arts, or humanities; or 8) research funded by any grant, contract, or otherwise by another person or governmental entity.

Did you make payments for basic research to a qualified institution? R&D tax credit includes 20% of the basic research payments to a qualified university, scientific research organization, scientific tax-exempt organization, or grant organization. This credit applies only to C corporations.

Did you make payments for energy research to to energy research consortia? R&D tax credit includes 20% of the energy research payments to energy research consortia, which is any organization organized and operated primarily to conduct energy research, and not a private foundation.

action_items.description: Make sure to figure your credit using both the regular method and the alternative simplified method for which you are eligible to get the maximum credit allowed.

If you do not elect the reduced credit under section 280C, reduce your otherwise allowable deduction for qualified research expenses or basic research expenses by the amount of the credit, and provide a statement that lists the deduction amounts that were reduced.

If you file as a member of a controlled group, attach a statement showing how your share of the credit was figured, the name and employer identification numbers or taxpayer identification numbers of the other members of the group, and the designated member. Write “See Attached” next to the entry space for line 17 or line 34.

name: Child Tax Credit
description: This tax credit may help you with the cost of raising kids.

qualification_criteria.question: Will each child be under age 17 at the end of the tax year?  To be a qualifying child, your dependent must be under age 17 at the end of the tax year.

Is each child your son, daughter, stepchild, eligible foster child, brother, sister, stepbrother, stepsister, half-brother, half-sister, or a descendant of one of these (for example, a grandchild, niece or nephew)?

Did you provide at least half of the financial support during the year for each child?  To qualify, each child cannot have provided more than half of his or her own financial support during the tax year.

Has each child lived with you more than half of the year?  To qualify, each child must live with you in the U.S. for more than half the year. The time that the child lives with you does not have to be consecutive.
Can each child be claimed as a dependent on your tax return?  Each child must be considered a dependent for tax filing purposes.
Does each child have a Social Security number that is valid for employment?  Each child must have a valid Social Security number.
Is each child a U.S. citizen, a U.S. National, or a U.S. resident alien?
Do you meet the income test?  You qualify for the full amount of the tax credit if your annual income is not more than $2000,000 for single filers or $400,000 for joint filers.

action_items.description: Make sure that each child has lived with you more than half of the year
Obtain valid Social Security numbers for each child,if they do not already have one.
Claim a child as your dependent in your tax return.

name: Coverdell ESA
description: You can save for your child's education while having tax-deferred growth of your assets and having tax-free distributions for qualified educational expenses.

qualification_criteria.question: Is the child under 18 years old? When the account is established, the child who will be the designated beneficiary of the Coverdell ESA must be under 18 years old. There can be exceptions to this rule if the child has special needs.

Will the contributions to the account be $2,000 or less per child per year? You can have more than one Coverdell account for a child, but the total contribution to all accounts for that child cannot exceed $2,000 per year, even if made by different contributors.

Is your modified adjusted gross income (MAGI) less than $95,000 per year, if filing as a single, or less than $190,000 per year, if married filing jointly? If your income is within these limits, you can contribute up to $2,000 into the Coverdell account. If your MAGI is above $110,000 as a single filer, or above $220,000 per year, if married filing jointly, you are not eligible to contribute to a Coverdell account. If your income falls between the lower and higher limits, the amount that you can contribute will be reduced. There are no income limits on organizations, such as corporations or trusts.

Do you plan on either distributing the funds in the Coverdell account by the time the child is 30 or rolling the account over to another child? If the child does not go to college by age 30 and does not use all of the funds for qualified education expenses, the funds will be forcibly distributed with a 10% penalty, and income tax will come due at that time. To avoid a penalty, the beneficiary of the account can be changed to someone under age 30 or the funds can be transferred to a Section 529 plan. In the event that the distributions are higher than the expenses, the gains are taxed at the beneficiary's rate, rather than the contributor's rate.

Will the educational expenses for which you want the funds used be considered qualified expenses? Qualified educational expenses include, but are not limited to: tuition, room and board, computers and laptops even if not required, books and supplies, tutoring, and transportation. The IRS takes a broad view of what are considered qualified educational expenses.

implementation_details.time_to_implement: 1-2 total hours to complete account set up paperwork and make contributions
implementation_details.third_party_details: A financial institution is needed to set up the Coverdell ESA for a designated beneficiary (child)

key_dates.deadline: 4/15/20XX - Contributions for the previous year must be made by the tax filing deadline

action_items.description: Set up the Coverdell ESA at a financial institution before the child turns 18 years old. The account must be designated as a Coverdell ESA when it is set up. Enrollment forms will need to be completed, including designating a beneficiary and a "responsible individual.

Make a cash contribution into the Coverdell ESA. The cash contribution can be invested in any qualifying investment (not life insurance) available at the financial institution where the ESA is set up.

tax_filing_by_entity.details: 1040 - Schedule 1 | 8z | Publication 970 | Beneficiary of Coverdell ESA | Use Part II of Form 5329 to report additional tax from a penalty if the withdrawal exceeds the qualified education expenses. The additional tax will then be reported on Form 1040, Schedule 2, line 8. Exceptions to the penalty include death, disability or if the beneficiary receives a qualified scholarship.

state_considerations: Contributions to a Coverdell ESA do not have to be reported on your federal return but may qualify for a deduction on your state return.

training_qa_pairs.source: AG FinTax Tax Strategies Knowledge Base

training_qa_pairs.question: How do I implement the Coverdell ESA strategy? What's involved?
training_qa_pairs.answer:
Time to implement: 1-2 total hours to complete account set up paperwork and make contributions
Third-party help needed: Yes — A financial institution is needed to set up the Coverdell ESA for a designated beneficiary (child)
Can be applied retroactively: No
training_qa_pairs.source: AG FinTax Tax Strategies Knowledge Base

training_qa_pairs.question: What are the deadlines for the Coverdell ESA strategy? When should I implement it?
training_qa_pairs.answer: Deadline: 4/15/20XX - Contributions for the previous year must be made by the tax filing deadline
training_qa_pairs.source: AG FinTax Tax Strategies Knowledge Base

training_qa_pairs.question: What are the step-by-step action items to implement the Coverdell ESA strategy?
training_qa_pairs.answer:
Here are the required steps to implement the Coverdell ESA strategy:
1. Set up the Coverdell ESA at a financial institution before the child turns 18 years old. The account must be designated as a Coverdell ESA when it is set up. Enrollment forms will need to be completed, including designating a beneficiary and a "responsible individual. (Mandatory)
2. Make a cash contribution into the Coverdell ESA. The cash contribution can be invested in any qualifying investment (not life insurance) available at the financial institution where the ESA is set up. (Mandatory)
training_qa_pairs.source: AG FinTax Tax Strategies Knowledge Base

training_qa_pairs.question: How do I report the Coverdell ESA strategy on my tax return? What forms are needed?
training_qa_pairs.answer:
The Coverdell ESA strategy must be properly recorded on your tax return. Here's how it's reported by entity type:
- Individual: 1040 - Schedule 1 | 8z | Publication 970 | Beneficiary of Coverdell ESA | Use Part II of Form 5329 to report additional tax from a penalty if the withdrawal exceeds the qualified education expenses. The additional tax will then be reported on Form 1040, Schedule 2, line 8. Exceptions to the penalty include death, disability or if the beneficiary receives a qualified scholarship.
training_qa_pairs.source: AG FinTax Tax Strategies Knowledge Base

training_qa_pairs.question: Are there any state-specific considerations for the Coverdell ESA strategy?
training_qa_pairs.answer: Contributions to a Coverdell ESA do not have to be reported on your federal return but may qualify for a deduction on your state return.

training_qa_pairs.question: Can the Coverdell ESA strategy be applied retroactively?
training_qa_pairs.answer: No, the Coverdell ESA strategy cannot be applied retroactively. It must be implemented before the applicable deadline.

name: Dependent Care Credit
description: This tax credit may help you pay for the care of eligible dependents.

qualification_criteria.question: Do you pay someone to care for a dependent who lives with you so that you can go to work, look for work, or go to school? If you pay someone to care for a dependent who is a “qualifying person” so that you can go to work, look for work, or go to school, you may be eligible to claim the Dependent Care tax credit.
Is the dependent who is receiving care under age 13?
Is the dependent your spouse, and is your spouse not able to mentally or physically care for themself, and did your spouse live in your home at least half of the year?
Is the dependent someone other than your child or spouse, and is the person not able to take care of himself or herself, and has the person lived in your home at least half of the year?
Did you or your spouse or do you or your spouse earn income from a job during the period that care was provided or is being provided?
Is you and your spouse's combined earned income less than $438,000 per year?

action_items.description: Obtain a caregiver
Obtain the name, address, and Taxpayer Identification Number of the person providing the care. IRS Form W-10
Keep a payment history of expenses for dependent care
If you are married, you must file a joint tax return.

tax_filing_by_entity.details: 1040, Schedule 3 | 13g | Publication 503 | Tax Advisor | Form 2441 must be completed and attached to Form 1040.

state_considerations: If the care provider works in your home, you may need to pay state unemployment taxes and may need to carry workers compensation insurance.

name: FICA Tip Credit
description: If you own a restaurant and pay tipped employees, you may be able to take a credit on part of the employer FICA taxes paid on the employee tips.

qualification_criteria.question: Do you have employees who receive tips for providing, delivering, or serving food or beverages to customers? Only businesses in the restaurant industry are eligible for the FICA tip credit.

Was the customer free to pay the tip, and did the customer have the right to determine who receives the tip? Automatic gratuities are not considered tips, so they must be treated like regular wages paid to an employee and cannot be used to determine the FICA tip credit.

Did you pay or incur employer Social Security and Medicare taxes on tips on at least one employee who earned more than $20/month in tips? Employers are allowed to take a credit against their federal income tax based on the amount of FICA and Medicare taxes paid on tips reported to the employer. Employees are required to report tips that are over $20/month.

implementation_details.time_to_implement: up to 3 hours for calculations, depending on how many tipped employees

action_items.description: Provide accounting records showing tips reported by employees and FICA taxes on those tips to interested parties.
Complete appropriate tax forms, depending on entity type.

tax_filing_by_entity.details: 1040, Schedule C | 6 | Form 8846 and Instructions | Tax Advisor | Form 8846 and Form 3800 must be filed with the business's federal tax return
1065, Schedule K | 15f | Form 8846 and Instructions | Tax Advisor | Form 8846 must be filed with the business's federal tax return
1120S, Schedule K | 13g | Form 8846 and Instructions | Tax Advisor | Form 8846 must be filed with the business's federal tax return
1120, Schedule J (Part I) | 5c | Form 8846 and Instructions | Tax Advisor | Form 8846 and Form 3800 must be filed with the business's federal tax return
1041, Schedule G | 2b | Form 8846 and Instructions | Tax Advisor | Form 8846 and Form 3800 must be filed with the business's federal tax return

state_considerations: If the state minimum wage rate is higher than the federal minimum wage rate, the higher rate applies. Employers must check the applicable state laws before claiming the tip credit. Some states don't allow the tip tax credit to be taken at all. Several other states permit a tip credit but require that workers be paid a higher cash wage than what is required under federal law.

Several other states permit a tip credit but require that workers be paid a higher cash wage than what is required under federal law.
training_qa_pairs.source: AG FinTax Tax Strategies Knowledge Base

training_qa_pairs.question: Can the FICA Tip Credit strategy be applied retroactively?
training_qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the FICA Tip Credit strategy can be applied retroactively. Consult your tax advisor for specific retroactive filing requirements.
training_qa_pairs.source: AG FinTax Tax Strategies Knowledge Base

name: Reasonable Compensation
description: Taxpayers need to consider their total “compensation” needs and how to best distribute that compensation across various types of income (wages, dividends, etc.).

qualification_criteria.question: Does the business plan to pay out compensation to their owners/shareholder employees?

implementation_details.time_to_implement: 1-2 hours to review position and assess compensation
implementation_details.retroactive_details: Yes, if filing Late S Election

action_items.description: Obtain description of services performed by the business owner/shareholder employee
Obtain the annual compensation planned to pay the business owner/shareholder employee.

Perform a "market approach" analysis to determine reasonableness.

"Under this approach, the determination of the reasonableness of an employee’s compensation is made by comparing the employee’s compensation with the compensation of employees performing similar duties at similar companies. Ideally, the companies for comparison would be mirror images of the company being analyzed." - IRS Valuation Professionals Aid
Adjust compensation based on the market approach's defined reasonableness for the services performed by the owner/shareholder employee.
Store Market Approach Analysis, job description, and confirmation of adjusted salary amount to store for recordkeeping purposes

training_qa_pairs.question: What are the step-by-step action items to implement the Reasonable Compensation strategy?
training_qa_pairs.answer:
Here are the required steps to implement the Reasonable Compensation strategy:
1. Obtain description of services performed by the business owner/shareholder employee (Mandatory)
2. Obtain the annual compensation planned to pay the business owner/shareholder employee. (Mandatory)
3. Perform a "market approach" analysis to determine reasonableness.

"Under this approach, the determination of the reasonableness of an employee’s compensation is made by comparing the employee’s compensation with the compensation of employees performing similar duties at similar companies. Ideally, the companies for comparison would be mirror images of the company being analyzed." - IRS Valuation Professionals Aid (Mandatory)
4. Adjust compensation based on the market approach's defined reasonableness for the services performed by the owner/shareholder employee. (Mandatory)
5. Store Market Approach Analysis, job description, and confirmation of adjusted salary amount to store for recordkeeping purposes (Optional)
training_qa_pairs.source: AG FinTax Tax Strategies Knowledge Base

training_qa_pairs.answer:
The Reasonable Compensation strategy must be properly recorded on your tax return. Here's how it's reported by entity type:
- Individual 1040 Only: 1040 | Tax Advisor
- 1040 Schedule C: Wages reported on 1040; deduction on Sch. C | Line 26 (Note: Sch. C's cannot pay wages to the owner) | Tax Advisor
- S Corp: Wages reported on 1040; deduction on 1120-s and K-1 | Line 1 of 1040; line 8 of 1120-s; line 1 of K-1 | IRS Valulation Professionals Aid | Tax Advisor
- C Corp: Wages reported on 1040; deduction on 1120; calculated on Form 1125-E | Line 1 of 1040; Line 12 of 1120 for compensation of officer | IRS Valulation Professionals Aid | Tax Advisor
- Partnership: Wages reported on 1040; deduction on 1065 and K-1 | Line 1 of 1040; line 10 of 1065; line 1 of K-1 (Note: Partnerships cannot typically pay wages to partners. Guaranteed payments are reported on line 10) | IRS Valulation Professionals Aid | Tax Advisor
